text:
To find out how many miles farther Shandy drove than Matteo, we need to calculate the distance each of them traveled and then find the difference between those distances.

First, let's calculate the distance traveled by Matteo:
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Matteo}} = \text{Speed}_{\text{Matteo}} \times \text{Time}_{\text{Matteo}} \]
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Matteo}} = 55 \, \text{miles/hour} \times 4 \, \text{hours} \]
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Matteo}} = 220 \, \text{miles} \]

Next, let's calculate the distance traveled by Shandy:
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Shandy}} = \text{Speed}_{\text{Shandy}} \times \text{Time}_{\text{Shandy}} \]
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Shandy}} = 45 \, \text{miles/hour} \times 10 \, \text{hours} \]
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Shandy}} = 450 \, \text{miles} \]

Now, we can find out how many miles farther Shandy drove than Matteo:
\[ \text{Difference} = \text{Distance}_{\text{Shandy}} - \text{Distance}_{\text{Matteo}} \]
\[ \text{Difference} = 450 \, \text{miles} - 220 \, \text{miles} \]
\[ \text{Difference} = 230 \, \text{miles} \]

So, Shandy drove 230 miles farther than Matteo.
